original_abstract: Accurate citywide traffic flow prediction is a cornerstone of intelligent transportation systems, yet existing modeling approaches often struggle to account for the complex heterogeneities inherent in urban data. Most current methods fail to address spatial heterogeneity—where skewed traffic distributions exist across different functional regions—and temporal heterogeneity, where traffic patterns evolve dynamically over time rather than following static cycles. To bridge these gaps, we propose Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL), a novel framework designed to enhance traffic pattern representations by explicitly modeling these variances. ST-SSL utilizes an integrated architecture of temporal and spatial convolutions to process flow data. To capture heterogeneity, the framework employs an adaptive graph augmentation scheme at both the attribute and structural levels, coupled with two auxiliary self-supervised learning tasks: a soft-clustering paradigm for spatial awareness and a contrastive learning objective for temporal dynamics. Rigorous experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate that ST-SSL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Our findings suggest that the proposed self-supervised approach effectively addresses the limitations of shared parameter spaces, offering a robust solution that may extend to other complex spatio-temporal applications.
humanized_abstract: Real-time traffic speed prediction is an essential part of smart traffic system. However, current traffic speed models cannot well handle the characteristics of spatial and temporal heterogeneities contained in urban traffic data. In this paper, we propose Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) framework to improve the representations of traffic speed patterns in urban scenes by fully considering the heterogeneities of traffic data. The framework processes the urban traffic speed data based on the integrated temporal and spatial convolution networks, and adopts an adaptive graph augmentation method for both attribute and structure variables. Meanwhile, it includes two self-supervised learning tasks such as the soft-clustering-based spatial aware branch and contrastive learning-based temporal aware branch. Experiment results on four urban traffic speed prediction datasets validated the effectiveness of the proposed framework, and showed that the models achieved higher prediction accuracy than those of other existing methods. The ST-SSL framework helps to deal with the negative effects brought by shared parameter spaces in models, and the improvement strategy can be applied to deal with the characteristics of other complex spatio-temporal data.
